title: The Rise of The Wonderful Company - YouTube
text:
Stewart and Linda Resnick are the
entrepreneurs behind the wonderful
company previously known as raw global
and roll international until 2015
they built an empire from the ground up
that now rakes in 4.6 billion dollars in
annual revenue it all started in 1979
when they bought a failing floral wire
service company Teleflora the Resnick's
tweaked the business model by adding
keepsake vases to the flowery delivery
and it was an immediate success they
then grew Teleflora through a number of
acquisitions until eventually it became
the largest florist Network in 1997
after acquiring the National florist
directory also known as Redbook
Teleflora is still an active acquirer
having bought Interflora UK in 2019 in
1979 the Resnick's also bought American
Protection industries or API a security
company that provides alarm monitoring
services at that time it was a wise move
as people in the US were becoming more
concerned about their physical safety in
the wake of riots and civil unrest API
eventually became the largest installer
of burglar alarms in the 1980's the
Resnick's then sold the company in 1989
for a hundred and five million dollars
to automated security holdings the
couple formed paramount sytrus
Association after they bought a sytrus
processing facility in California in
1981 and moving into the agribusiness
was a smartest move they made
in 1986 and 1987 they purchased
thousands of acres of land from oil
companies such as Mobil Oil and Texaco
and repurposed the land for pistachio
and almond farming operations they thus
formed paramount farms in 1989 to house
their nuts operations from their two
brands were born wonderful pistachios
and wonderful almonds they're actually
the world's largest grower and processor
of pistachios and almonds delivering 450
million pounds of these nuts annually in
1986 they bought Franklin Mint a company
that produced coins medals and other
collectibles the Resnick's turned the
company around by partnering with
designers such as Givenchy and McFadden
and even partnered with museums to
create collectible art under their reign
sales grew from 250 million dollars in
1985 to over 600 million dollars in 1995
in the late 1990s the business was sued
by Tiger Woods and the charitable
foundation of Princess Diana over what
essentially boils down to licensing
rights
the couple sold Franklin Mint in 2006 to
a group of private investors in 1996 the
power couple commissioned a study on the
health benefits of pomegranate when the
research showed positive results they
started to build a business around it
and launched the palm wonderful juice in
2002 revenues grew from 12 million
dollars in 2000 to 291 million dollars
in 2003 and reached a hundred and sixty
five million by 2007
however they ran into trouble with the
FTC which found that Palme was making
false and unsubstantiated claims about
their products health benefits over the
years Palme launched different product
lines such as ice coffee tea fruit bars
and supplements but it ultimately stuck
to only their line of pomegranate juice
in 2004 the Resnick's bought Fiji water
a premium bottled water brand the couple
managed to make Fiji the number one
premium bottled water brand in the US
outselling Avion in 2008 at first Fiji
water was paying a tax of one third of
cent per liter but when the government
raised the tax to 15 cents per liter
Fiji water along with nine other
companies cease operations in protest
they claim that the tax would undermine
the industry and they would need to lay
off thousands of employees to make up
for the increase in taxes needless to
say the government dropped the new tax
initiative the brand also faced
criticism over its marketing campaign
that read every drop is green and over
its carbon negative claims over the
years Fiji water expanded into the wine
business by acquiring Justin and
landmark vineyard and launching the jsq
brand in 2013 the Resnick's launched
wonderful halos mandarins they are
actually the largest integrated grower
of fresh citrus in the US they're
wonderful citrus division houses other
types of citrus products such as
grapefruit lemons limes and oranges in
summary the wonderful company has built
a portfolio of top brands in America
they even have their own in-house sales
and marketing agency that
services their portfolio of brands they
set up the wonderful real estate arm
that is active in the industrial real
estate space in California they are
developing three industrial business
parks that leases space to Fortune 500
companies such as Target and FedEx apart
from that they own Neptune Pacific Line
a shipping company and cetera a pest
control company but they can't be all
that wonderful right they face their
share of criticism over the massive
water consumption for their nuts
operation and their ownership of the
current water bank the water bank which
served as an emergency water supply was
originally state-owned until it was
turned over to farmers including the
Resnick's at the time it seemed like a
fair deal
since the farmers gave up most of their
paper water rights during that exchange
however the Resnick's who owned the
majority of the water bank use the
reservoir to store water they bought
from the government to later sell it
back at a premium in times of drought
with all that in mind what are your
thoughts about the wonderful company
have you ever bought their products did
you know about their portfolio of brands
let us know
